message:
refactor(raptorq): Simplify Gaussian elimination buffer management

Removes pre-allocated reusable buffers in InactivationDecoder's Gaussian
elimination, replacing with per-iteration cloning:

Before: Pre-allocate pivot_row_buf and pivot_rhs_buf once, copy data each
        iteration to avoid borrow conflicts

After:  Clone pivot row data directly each iteration

The change simplifies the code at the cost of additional allocations per
pivot operation. For typical RaptorQ block sizes (K < 8192), the reduced
complexity outweighs the marginal allocation overhead. The Gaussian
elimination phase is not the hot path - encoding/decoding symbol XOR
operations dominate runtime.

Also removes std::mem::take optimization in solution extraction, using
straightforward clone for clarity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
